subject: Physics
title: We Are Made of Stardust
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: We Are Made of Stardust.

hook.text: Every atom of carbon in your body was forged inside a star that exploded billions of years ago.

buildup.text: The Big Bang made only hydrogen and helium. Everything heavier was cooked inside stellar cores.

discovery.text: Stars fuse hydrogen into helium, then helium into carbon, oxygen, and heavier elements up to iron.

twist.text: The calcium in your bones, the iron in your blood, and the oxygen you breathe—all from ancient supernovae.

climax.text: Carl Sagan said it best: 'We are a way for the cosmos to know itself.'

punchline.text: Stars lived and died so you could exist. You are literally stardust.

quiz.question: Where were the heavy elements in your body created?
options: Inside stars that later exploded as supernovae
